page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture compute-optimized server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical Intel Xeon 8369B cores running at a clock speed of 2.9 GHz. The processor includes an L3 cache of 48 MB, alongside L1d and L2 caches of 48 KB and 1 MB respectively. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations position the ecs.c7se.2xlarge in the average tier across most standard compute and memory operations. The Intel Xeon processor delivers a single-core stress-ng score of 1854.49 and a multi-core score of 7765.29. Cryptographic and vector instruction capabilities are supported by hardware-accelerated flags such as AVX-512 and AES. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only performance of 419.97 GB/sec for small cached blocks, dropping to 60.18 GB/sec for larger uncached blocks, while write bandwidth reaches 42.4 GB/sec. In application-specific workloads, the instance achieves 8.0 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a static web serving throughput of 11.83 GB/sec.

The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. However, memory-intensive applications or large-scale LLM inference tasks present clear performance bottlenecks; for example, LLM inference speeds drop to a poor 15.94 tokens per second for prompt processing on a medium 7B model. Consequently, the ecs.c7se.2xlarge is best suited for workloads such as software compilation, web serving, lightweight database operations, and multi-threaded data decompression.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.2xlarge is a compute-optimized x86_64 server instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B physical cores running at 2.9 GHz and 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute and memory operations, with a single-core Geekbench score of 1574.0 and a multi-core score of 5909.0. The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in software compilation and static web serving, achieving 11.83 GB/sec throughput. However, the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and lack of GPU acceleration limit its effectiveness for memory-intensive databases or medium-to-large language model inference. It is highly suited for compute-bound workloads, web serving, and multi-threaded decompression tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 16 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering balanced mid-range performance for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 3 Gbps network baseline.
tagline: A dedicated eight-core compute-optimized instance delivering balanced processing power and high-speed vector instructions for standard web and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B physical cores running at 2.9 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Hardware-accelerated AVX-512, AES, and SHA instruction sets
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and decompression tasks
Lacks local storage and dedicated GPU accelerators
